methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing water samples for analysis of environmental variables and LOFA extraction.

steps.description: Samplings were taken weekly from January to December 2017 in the GoN, at the Long-Term Ecological-Research Station MareChiara (LTER-MC; 40°48.5′N, 14°15′E).
steps.searchable_summary: collect weekly water samples from LTER-MC station

steps.description: Water samples were collected at surface (0.5 m) with 10 L plastic buckets and pre-filtered onto a 200 µm mesh nylon net to remove large debris and mesozooplankton.
steps.searchable_summary: pre-filter water samples to remove debris

steps.description: 250 mL were transferred into brown-glass bottles, fixed with formaldehyde (final concentration of 1.6%) and stored at 4°C in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and store water samples with formaldehyde

steps.description: For LOFA analysis, a variable volume (0.5-2 L) of the same seawater was filtered onto Millipore polycarbonate filters using a multiple vacuum filtration apparatus.
Filters were immediately frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at −80°C.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze and store filters for LOFA analysis

methods.description: Method for identifying and counting surface phytoplankton using microscopy.

steps.description: Surface phytoplankton was identified and counted with a Zeiss Axiovert200 inverted microscope following the Utermöhl method.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count phytoplankton using microscopy

steps.description: Depending on cell concentration, subsamples of 3-50 mL were settled and cells were counted on one/two transects at ×400 magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: count cells in subsamples at high magnification

steps.description: Larger (>20 µm) and less abundant species were quantified in half of the chamber at lower magnification (×200).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify larger species at lower magnification

steps.description: Abundance was expressed as cells/L. For biomass calculation, average bio-volumes were converted to carbon using formulas.

methods.description: Procedure for extracting and quantifying LOFAs from filtered seawater samples.

steps.description: 1 mL of Milli-Q water was added to frozen filters. Samples were sonicated and left for 30 min at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: add water and sonicate samples for LOFA extraction

steps.description: 16-Hydroxyhexadecanoic acid (1 µg) was added as internal standard.
Samples were methylated with diazomethane, dried under nitrogen flow and re-dissolved in methanol.
steps.searchable_summary: methylate and prepare samples for analysis

steps.description: Targeted LOFAs were quantified in ESI + mode through Q Exactive Hybrid Quadrupole Orbitrap equipped with an HPLC-System.
Absolute amount of each LOFA was normalized by the volume of water filtered and expressed as ng-LOFAs/L.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize LOFA amounts by water volume

methods.description: Analysis of diatom taxa interactions using network analysis to identify modules and characterize connections.

steps.description: Network analysis clustered 52 out of 53 nodes, i.e. diatom taxa, in three distinct modules.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster diatom taxa into modules using network analysis

steps.description: Categorization of edges on the basis of fg-LOFAs/diatom cell highlighted maximal densities of HTrA-derived LOFAs.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize network edges based on LOFA densities

methods.description: Statistical analyses to estimate monthly variations of LOFAs and identify main contributors to variations.

steps.description: Monthly variations of LOFAs were estimated through a one-way analysis of similarities (ANOSIM) test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOSIM test for LOFA variations

steps.description: Similarity percentages (SIMPER) analysis allowed discerning main contributors of variations among classes of objects.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SIMPER analysis to identify variation contributors
